summary: A Ukrainian drone attack struck the middle superstructure of the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen" in Novorossiysk Bay, detonating PK-10 countermeasure grenade launchers and damaging the TK-25 electronic warfare complex. Ukrainian operators conducted FPV strikes 50+ km into occupied Donetsk, hitting military and logistics vehicles. Russian forces using the "Rubikon" Center reported intercepting over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with 75% belonging to 11 specific models, and destroyed 107 targets on March 6. Ukrainian company Vyriy developed new "Lightning" UAV versions with alternative control systems and retargeting capabilities. Russian forces adapted by equipping fixed-wing UAVs to carry two FPV drones each, prompting calls for enhanced anti-drone defenses. Ukrainian forces used ZALA Lancet loitering munitions to strike group targets despite electronic warfare interference, and FAB-3000 bombs with UMPK were deployed against personnel in Krasny Liman, captured via optical fiber FPV drone.
